channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Horus Heresy Legio Custodes Venatari Sodality and Coronus Grav-Carrier Review - Golden Reinforcements
item.content:encoded:
The Emperor, one of the most powerful beings in the Warhammer 40,000 universe, needs a suitably superpowered bodyguard, and those come in the form of the Legio Custodes, embodiments of the Emperor's will, and unlike the Adeptus Astartes, their loyalty is unquestioned. These super beings have been serving the Emperor since before the Unification Wars, when the Emperor came to power and united Terra under his rule. These superlative warriors have just received an update for the Horus Heresy wargame, the gaming system set 10,000 years before Warhammer 40K, and following the release of their Liber Custodes book and Battle Group, they get some brand new units. In this article, we check out the new Legio Custodes Venatari Sodality and Coronus Grav-Carrier.

Want to know more about the Horus Heresy? You can check out our Guide or review of the system.

Golden Sentinels

The Legio Custodes are genetically-engineered super-soldiers, each created to be an extremely capable warrior for the sole purpose of protecting the Emperor. They differ from the Space Marines, who are created to work in units with overwhelming force to defeat their enemies, the Custodes are created as individual warriors, with extremely high levels of intelligence in order to best enact the defense of the Emperor. 

The Legio Custodes also work alongside the Anathema Psykana (also known as the Sisters of Silence) and the Divisio Assassinorum, and are collectively known as the Talons of the Emperor.

Legio Custodes Battle Group

Coinciding with the release of the Liber Custodes, the faction book specifically for the Legio Custodes (and the wider Talons of the Emperor forces), the Legio Custodes got some brand new units that were boxed together in the Legio Custodes Battle Group. This battle group box and the Liber Custodes book are the perfect place to start with a Custodes force, giving you the backbone from which to create your force. The units from that box are also releasing separately with the new units we detail below, so if you can't find that Battle Group box, you can still start to build your force with the units from that box.

The Legio Custodes Battle Group box contains:

  • 1x Shield Captain
  • 6x Custodian Guard
  • 6x Sentinel Guard
  • 1x Contemptor-Achillus Dreadnought that can also be built as a Contemptor-Galatus Dreadnought
  • 1x Caladius Annihilator grav-tank

The first of the new units for the Legio Custodes is the Venatari Sodality. The Venatari are elite jump-pack troops whose packs feature golden wings, giving them an angelic profile. Their jump packs give them a move of 14 inches, and their lighter armor gives them a 3+ save, whereas their more heavily armored counterparts usually have a 2+ save.

They come in a box of 6, with 2 armament options, either a pistol and shield combo, a Kinetic Destroyer and Tarsus Buckler, or melee-focused with Verutum Lance. The buckler helps to reduce the chances of the Venatari being wounded when receiving volley fire or overwatch, which is usually the precursor to them being charged or them charging a unit, meaning they're more likely to get into combat. But once in combat, the Verutum Lance is a lot more effective, so your choice will depend on the battlefield target you want for them. There are enough weapons in the box to let you arm all 6 Venatari the same way, or a mixture depending on your needs.

The Venatari Sodality miniatures look awesome. I'm a Raven Guard player in 40K, so the wings and the slightly beaky mouth-plates make me jealous. You'll be deploying them with a specific battlefield target in mind, armed with Neutronium mines for vehicles, Kinetic Destroyer for troops, or Verutum Lance for armored targets, and their fast movement is great for getting into your opponents' lines to get to those targets.

Coronus Grav-Carrier

Durable as the Adeptus Custodes are, concentrated fire or powerful weapons can still get through their golden armor, so adding an extra level of protection to get them to their targets will help your plans, and the Coronus Grav-Carrier is just that. It's not super durable, but it does have a move of 12, and the extra level of protection it adds to your Custodes is welcome. But it does come backed by some serious weapons. It's got a forward-mounted Twin Lastrum Bolt Cannon, but the real damage dealer is the turret Twin Arachnus Blaze Cannon that has 2 fire settings depending on your target, armor, or troops.

The Coronus Grav-Carrier can transport 7 Custodes, usually a unit of 6 and a character, and the model feels suitably bulky enough for that, with a longer chassis than the Grav-Tank counterparts. In-game, it acts like a light troop insertion platform, with a powerful weapon, rather than a heavy tank, so be prepared to disembark you Custodes as soon as it's in position and use the Coronus' weapons to cover their advance.

10,000 Years Protecting The Emperor

The Adeptus Cusodes are still serving the Emperor, with unwavering loyalty, 10,000 years after the events of the Horus Heresy in Warhammer 40K. It's a testament to the quality of their armaments that they still use the same weapons and armor in both systems, and it's great for Custodes players because they can use the miniatures across both systems.

Both the Venatari Sodality and the Coronus Grav-Carrier have rules for Warhammer 40K under Imperial Armor that can be found in the Downloads sections of the Warhammer Community site. Imperial Armor datasheets are generally for units that were produced in Forge World resin, and Games Workshop hinted when these miniatures were first revealed in the New Year's Preview that there would be an update to the Adeptus Custodes faction pack, so hopefully we'll get updated rules for the Venatari and Grav Carrier to match their prowess in the Horus Heresy.

Horus Heresy Legio Custodes Venatari Sodality and Coronus Grav-Carrier Final Thoughts?

Both the Venatari Sodality and Coronus Grav-Carrier fill a needed role for the Legio Custodes in the Horus Heresy, and will be a welcome addition to expand the range in 40K if they get updated datasheets. The miniatures are glorious, and I especially like the lighter armored look of the Venatari (along with their glorious wings). 

The Custodes in both systems are a force to be reckoned with. They don't have a lot of units, so they are great for beginners to learn and their power and durablity makes them forgiving. Their small number of units on the tabletop makes them easy to pilot and gives them a good learning curve, as you will have to learn the best and most efficient use of each unit to master them.

The copies of Horus Heresy Legio Custodes Venatari Sodality and Coronus Grav-Carrier used to produce this review were provided by Games Workshop. The copy of the Horus Heresy Legio Custodes Battle Group used to produce this review was purchased by the author.

Adam is a Tabletop Specialist for TechRaptor. He started writing for TechRaptor in 2017 and took over as Tabletop Editor in 2019 and has since stood down… More about Adam



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/horus-heresy-adeptus-custodes-venatari-sodality-and-coronus-grav-carrier-review
